scored_claims.claim: Agrium's Operational Excellence program—targeting $475M in cumulative cost savings by 2020 with over 50% achieved by end of 2014—combined with a natural gas hedging program securing approximately 80% of Q1 2015 needs and 25-30% of 2016-2018 needs at approximately $3/MMBtu, demonstrates a systematic cost management capability that enhances margin resilience in commodity downturns.
scored_claims.evidence: Q4 2014 call: 'Operational Excellence program targeting $475M; more than 50% has been achieved by end of 2014.' Natural gas hedging: '80% of Q1 needs hedged; approximately 25-30% of 2016-2018 needs at approximately $3/MMBtu.' Logistics optimization: repatriated 150,000 tonnes of nitrogen and phosphate from US to Western Canada using own distribution network—example of operational excellence in action.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Operational excellence programs are legitimate but generally replicable by any major operator with sufficient capital and management focus. Natural gas hedging provides time-limited protection but is not a structural competitive advantage. 50% confidence.

scored_claims.claim: Agrium's capital allocation strategy—increasing dividend payout ratio to 40-50% of free cash flow net of sustaining capital, with a $7B+ aggregate free cash flow potential over 2016-2020 underpinned by Vanscoy potash, Borger nitrogen, and Retail growth—represents a management commitment to returning capital that is predicated on the competitive advantage of the integrated business model.
scored_claims.evidence: Q4 2014 call: Management guided to '$7B+ in free cash flow over 5 years starting 2016' and increased dividend payout ratio to '40-50% of FCF net of sustaining capital.' Annual EPS guidance introduced: '$7.00-$8.50 for 2015.' These commitments presuppose sustained competitive advantage in both Wholesale (new capacity) and Retail (tuck-in growth).
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: This claim fails Rule08: management's commitment to returning $7B+ in FCF over 5 years is a consequence of their assumed competitive position, not an advantage itself. Lowered to 30% confidence. Including as evidence of management's confidence in their competitive model, but not a standalone competitive advantage.

scored_claims.claim: Agrium delivered record Retail EBITDA of $1.12B in 2014 (+17% comparable YoY), generated approximately $750M in free cash flow, and guided to EPS of $7.00-$8.50 for 2015, demonstrating sustained earnings power of the integrated Wholesale-Retail model through agricultural commodity price cycles.
scored_claims.evidence: Q4 2014 call: Record Retail EBITDA $1.12B, +17% on comparable basis. Free cash flow approximately $750M in 2014. EPS guidance for 2015: $7.00-$8.50. 2013 full-year Retail EBITDA $961M was also a record at that time. Consecutive records in Retail EBITDA despite macro headwinds (shortened fall season, -25%+ nutrient prices in 2013) support durability claim.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Consecutive records in Retail EBITDA with quantified evidence across multiple years during mixed commodity cycles is strong performance evidence. 70% confidence for cagr_connection.

scored_claims.claim: Agrium reported full-year 2013 Retail EBITDA of $961M and full-year 2014 Retail EBITDA of $1.12B. The company had customer prepayments of $1.7B (2013, +6% YoY) and generated approximately $750M in free cash flow in 2014. EPS guidance for 2015 was $7.00-$8.50.
scored_claims.evidence: Directly reported in Q4 2013 and Q4 2014 earnings call transcripts. These are reported financial results and management-issued guidance, not claims requiring interpretation.
scored_claims.scoring_rationale: Directly reported financial results from earnings calls. 90% confidence for factual baseline.
